lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Ah [player name], glad to see you made it. Welcome to Lletya, home to the Elven Resistance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. It wasn't particularly easy to get here, it took some work to convince Islwyn I could be trusted.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: That's not surprising, he is not particularly fond of humans. But if you've earnt his trust, it proves to me that I was right to take a chance on you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm glad you did. I hate the thought that I might still be blindly working for King Lathas and Lord Iorwerth right now.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Well you're here now and you still have a chance to right your previous wrongs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hope so. There's a lot I still don't understand though. Why are King Lathas and Lord Iorwerth working together? Where does the plague fit in?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I'm afraid that a lot is still unknown to us as well. However, I will explain what I can. Be aware that this is only a brief overview. You'll find some books around the village that explain more if you wish though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, let's begin.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Many years ago, all elves lived in harmony within the crystal city of Prifddinas alongside our goddess, Seren. When the God Wars ended, Seren was lost to us. For the first time, we were alone. To lead us through those difficult times, Baxtorian Cadarn was named the first king of the elves. Under Baxtorian, our kingdom expanded into the east. We prospered for a time, but little did we know that an enemy walked among us. During that time, Lord Iorwerth turned away from Seren and pledged himself to Zamorak, the Dark Lord. A larger kingdom meant our forces were further spread. Lord Iorwerth took advantage of that. He claimed Prifddinas as his own. Baxtorian tried to take it back from him, but Prifddinas was built to survive the God Wars. There was no way to retake it. In a last attempt to keep the city safe, Baxtorian and the clan elders sung the song of inversion which reverted the entire city into a crystal seed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, so that explains why the city just looks like an empty field.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Indeed. After that, Baxtorian returned to the east but found that the rest of the kingdom had fallen while he was away. Not only that, but his wife, Glarial, had been taken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know this bit, Baxtorian retreated into his home beneath the waterfall and never came out. I moved Glarial's remains into the waterfall so they could be together.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: That's good to hear, I can see why you managed to earn Islwyn's trust. Anyway, with Baxtorian gone and Prifddinas lost, our land erupted into a Civil War. We've been fighting ever since.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see, it's good to have a bit more understanding. But where does King Lathas come into this?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: That is the bit we're not so sure on. We know little about the lands to the east. From what we can tell, it seems that Lord Iorwerth has promised to support King Lathas in reclaiming some land his family once owned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why? What's in it for Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: That's what we want you to find out. According to our spies, Lord Iorwerth has used the alliance with King Lathas as an opportunity to infiltrate West Ardougne. The city must contain something he needs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Infiltrate? How?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Those that you know as mourners are in fact elves in service to Lord Iorwerth. We regularly see them crossing the Arandar mountain pass and into the east.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mourners are elves?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: That they are. We assume that the plague is a cover up for them to enter the city unchallenged. We don't know why though. That's why we need you. You know the city and can freely travel there. You are the perfect person for this task. We need you to go to West Ardougne, infiltrate the mourners and find out what they are doing in the city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's skip the backstory.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I'd argue that understanding is essential to what comes next. But as you wish.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: The bit that is relevant to you is the recent alliance between King Lathas and Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: From what we can tell, it seems that Lord Iorwerth has promised to support King Lathas in reclaiming some land his family once owned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why? What's in it for Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: That's what we want you to find out. According to our spies, Lord Iorwerth has used the alliance with King Lathas as an opportunity to infiltrate West Ardougne. The city must contain something he needs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Infiltrate? How?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Those that you know as mourners are in fact elves in service to Lord Iorwerth. We regularly see them crossing the Arandar mountain pass and into the east.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mourners are elves?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: That they are. We assume that the plague is a cover up for them to enter the city unchallenged. We don't know why though. That's why we need you.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: You know the city and can freely travel there. You are the perfect person for this task. We need you to go to West Ardougne, infiltrate the mourners and find out what they are doing in the city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you want me to infiltrate the mourners? How am I supposed to achieve that?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I don't have an exact plan for you I'm afraid. You know the city better than any of us so are best placed to make a plan yourself. However as I said before, there have been sightings of mourners crossing the Arandar mountain pass. I'm sure you can take advantage of that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: This will cut down you to size!

sections.title: Asking Arianwyn for help with the bloody top and ripped trousers

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I killed one of those mourners crossing the mountain pass and took their clothes.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Ah, a disguise. Good thinking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do have a bit of an issue though. The top has blood on it and the trousers are ripped.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Hmm... Well you can try asking our local seamstress, Oronwen, about the trousers. As for the top, you'll need to find a way to get it cleaned. Maybe you can find someone who does a lot of laundry. Someone who wears lots of white might be a good bet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks for the help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, you're doing laundry, eh?

lines.speaker: Tegid
lines.text: Yes. What is it to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nice day for it.

lines.speaker: Tegid
lines.text: I suppose it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know any way to remove blood stains?

lines.speaker: Tegid
lines.text: Blood stains is it... Well, the soap I use can clean almost any stain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really?!? That sounds like just the thing I need, can I use some?

lines.speaker: Tegid
lines.text: No, I don't have very much soap left and I still have lots to get clean.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright can you tell me where I can buy some?

lines.speaker: Tegid
lines.text: You can't... I make it to my own secret recipe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you be less helpful?

lines.speaker: Tegid
lines.text: Like you were, taking those robes from my washing line?? So yes, if you know a way I can be less helpful just tell me. I'll try it.

sections.title: Use Tegid's soap with the bloody mourner top
Talking to Oronwen before giving the items

lines.speaker: Oronwen
lines.text: Hello, can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you need to mend my trousers?

lines.speaker: Oronwen
lines.text: I need bear fur and two pieces of silk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: Oronwen
lines.text: Hello, can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have all I need to mend my trousers.

lines.speaker: Oronwen
lines.text: Pass it all here then, and I will get started.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks a lot.

lines.speaker: Oronwen
lines.text: Any time.

lines.speaker: Oronwen
lines.text: I need bear fur and one piece of silk.

lines.speaker: Oronwen
lines.text: I only need bear fur now.

lines.speaker: Oronwen
lines.text: I need two pieces of silk.

lines.speaker: Oronwen
lines.text: I only need one piece of silk.

lines.speaker: Oronwen
lines.text: Hello, can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you mend clothes?

lines.speaker: Oronwen
lines.text: I do, but human clothes are too hard to mend. They do not have the finesse of elven garments.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need you to repair some elven clothing as it goes.

lines.speaker: Oronwen
lines.text: Let me take a look at it then.

lines.speaker: Oronwen
lines.text: There's something disturbingly familiar about the design of these trousers, they are made for an elf.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But can you fix them?

lines.speaker: Oronwen
lines.text: Of course I can, but I will need two pieces of silk and some bear fur.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, I have those here.

lines.speaker: Oronwen
lines.text: Huh, it's almost like you knew what I needed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks a lot.

lines.speaker: Oronwen
lines.text: Any time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I killed one of those mourners crossing the mountain pass and took their clothes.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Ah, a disguise. Good thinking. Use it to enter the Mourner Headquarters in West Ardougne. Once your in, see if you can find out what they are doing in the city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, will do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: It is good to see so many new recruits, soon no one will be able to stand against the power of the Death Guard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds... err great.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Indeed it is. You should speak with Essyllt downstairs. He's in charge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Good day. Are you in need of assistance?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Are the townspeople causing you trouble?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but I don't think you can help

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: You will be surprised at how much help the brute force of the Guard can be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'll be sure to ask if I'm in need of some muscle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I just wanted to talk to a friendly face.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Do I look friendly to you? I really must work on my scowl more.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Have you finished with those sheep yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm sorry I lost the gnomic device and the key.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Well it is lucky we have a whole stack of broken devices, there must be some flaw in the design, they always break in the same way. If you ever need another there is a chest full of them over there.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: I also keep copies of the key on my desk so I can replace that too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, I lost the gnomic device.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Well it is lucky we have a whole stack of broken ones, there must be some flaw in the design, they always break in the same way. If you ever need another there is a chest full of them over there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry but I lost the key.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: I keep copies of the key on my desk so I can replace that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, I'm...

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Ah... I take it you are one of the new recruits?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm...

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: I'm Essyllt and I'm in charge here. Now come on, let us have a look at your paper work.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: This seems to be all in order. Welcome to the Death Guard. Now, for your first assignment... As you may know, part of what we do here is keep the people believing in the plague...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Did they not tell you? Hmm... I'd better fill you in. Well as you undoubtedly know, we were sent here by Lord Iorwerth to secure the city. The reason for this is so that we can access the caves below...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's important about the caves?

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: I'm afraid that information is classified until you prove your commitment to the Death Guard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, fair enough.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Now to keep our presence in the city secret, Lord Iorwerth arranged an alliance with King Lathas, the ruler in the east. Apparently the fool thinks we will help him dispose of the Knights of Camelot once our work here is done. I expect he'll be disappointed. Anyway, the plague serves two purposes. First, it allows us to move around the city in secret thanks to our mourner disguises. Second, it means we can abduct citizens to mine the caves below with ease. We just pretend they've fallen victim to the plague.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Doesn't anyone suspect the truth?

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Well we have had a few near misses. Recently we had some girl from East Ardougne poking around asking too many questions, so we locked her up while we decided what to do with her. Annoyingly, she managed to escape with the help of some adventurer. Lucky for us, they were so stupid that we managed to use the adventurer to deal with King Tyras. It all worked out quite well in the end. Things like that is why we must ensure the people keep believing in the plague though. One of the things we do to keep up the lie is to fool old Farmer Brumty into believing his sheep are infected. The old halfwit thinks just because his sheep are an abnormal colour, that they are all ill with plague. It's amazing what a bit of dye can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You dyed them?

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Well you don't think they end up those ridiculous colours naturally do you? Anyway, shame of it is that we have yet to find a way to stop the dye from washing out. So we need someone, that someone being you, to go and re-dye them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Simple enough, you want me to give a blue rinse to a load of old sheep?

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Not quite, for starters the sheep need to be dyed red, yellow, green and blue. But most important is that you are not seen doing this by anyone. Also you will need to re-dye them the colours they already are or the farmer may notice the change.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds a little more tricky. How did you do it before?

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: We have a gnomic device that fires fat dye parcels that rupture on impact. Unfortunately, we have run out of the parcels and the device is broken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I take a look at it?

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Sure. We have a gnome inventor here too. Sadly, he is not being very helpful about fixing it, but you can talk to him if you like. Here is the key, he is in the next room. And here's the device as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, will you help me fix this... err... thing?

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: I'm not helping you fix that, as if it hasn't caused enough trouble as it is. Your friends have already tried every torture in the book! I'm still not telling anyone squat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have they tried... err... Stretching your eyelids yet?

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: Yes, it didn't work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about... feeding you nail and prune stew?

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: That's all I've been living on since I got here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Set fire to your nostril hair? Let rabid rabbits nibble your toes? Given you a twisted arm?

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: Yes, yes and yes. Tried them all, quite liked the toe nibbling.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Extracted your wisdom teeth?

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: Us gnomes aren't wise so we don't get them. Face it, you'll never put me in enough pain that I'll tell you what you wanna know. I used to play gnomeball as a kid. This is a walk in the park in comparison.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright I get the picture. What will work then?

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: Ha! You think I'm stupid enough to tell you that I've been craving toad crunchies or that I can't stand having my feet tickled!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err... But you just told me?!?

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: I did? What did I say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You said about going to the park and playing gnomeball.

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: Yes yes!! That was it, I'd hate that. Please don't do that. By Guthix's beard you mourners are such idiots! Leave me be moron.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You said about toad crunchies and a gnome ball.

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: I used to play gnomeball yes.... what's that got to do with anything? Nothing that can convince me to help you that's for sure. Go away imbecile.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You said about toad crunchies and being tickled.

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: Oops... I mean erm... No that must have been some other err... gnome...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're not fooling me... So you'll help me in exchange for toad crunchies?

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: If you think you can just buy my co-operation you're a bigger imbecile than I thought.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You will tell me, sooner or later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You said about being tickled and a gnome ball.

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: Oooh... a big scary gnomeball. I mean, it might crush me and my entire family, because we're so small!! They have big teeth and everything don't they? Sheesh. You mourners will have to try better than that. Now go away. I need some sleep after all these *yawn* scary threats.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You said about being tickled and going to the park.

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: No... I said being here is a walk in the park. Where do they get you guys? You must all be Ogre rejects for being too thick. Leave me be idiot.

sections.title: Tickling the gnome's feet with a feather

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now will you help me or would you prefer it if I tickle your feet?

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: Alright... alright! I'm beaten! Bring me some soft leather and some magic logs and I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have all of that here.

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: Well let me up off of this rack and I'll get started, I can't do anything while I'm all tied up.

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: You got everything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still need some soft leather.

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: Well when you have it all I'll fix that thing, but please don't tickle me any more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still need some magic logs.

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: Well when you have it all I'll fix that thing, but please don't tickle me any more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have it all here.

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: Well let me up off of this rack and I'll get started, I can't do anything while I'm all tied up.

sections.title: Using a feather on the gnome again without toad crunchies

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now will you help me or do I have to tickle your feet some more?

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: I told you, I'm not going to get anything out of helping you, am I? As I figure, that device is the only thing keeping me out of the slave pens, where I'd get fed worse than nail and prune stew!

sections.title: Tickle the gnome on rack again

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: Oi! There's no need for that, I already agreed to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's all well and good but I'm enjoying doing it.

lines.speaker: Gnome on a rack
lines.text: Ho ho ho... no more... Teehee... please stop... Hoohoo... he he he... I hate you!

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: What do you think you are doing? You are meant to be getting information out of him, not making friends!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to let him up if he's to fix this advice.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Okay, but without the proper paperwork he stays in this room.

lines.speaker: Gnome
lines.text: Right, here you go. Now leave me to eat my crunchies in peace.

lines.speaker: Gnome
lines.text: What are you after now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where I can get the dye parcels for his thing?

lines.speaker: Gnome
lines.text: You mourners are really not very good at this are you? Fine, since you gave me some crunchies, the parcels are actually toads.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Toads?

lines.speaker: Gnome
lines.text: Yes, toads. Get some bellows and fill them with the dye you want. Then just find a toad and use the bellows to fill it up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Poor toads.

lines.speaker: Gnome
lines.text: Ahh they're fine, a bit of dye never hurt anything... It's the firing them out the device that kills them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ewww... That's nasty.

lines.speaker: Gnome
lines.text: Oh and torturing gnomes is perfectly fine? Hypocrite.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You make a fair point. Where do I find the toads?

lines.speaker: Gnome
lines.text: You get loads of them down in the Feldip Hills, especially near the ponds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes, I've used them to catch chompy birds before. I have some bellows right here as well.

lines.speaker: Gnome
lines.text: Catching chompy birds? You mourners are weird. Anyway, if you don't need anything else I'd like to be left in peace.

lines.speaker: Gnome
lines.text: What are you after now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you remind me where I can get the dye parcels for this thing?

lines.speaker: Gnome
lines.text: How forgetful are you? Get some bellows and fill them with the dye you want. Then just head down to the Feldip Hills, find a toad and use the bellows to fill it up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the reminder. I have some bellows right here that I can use.

lines.speaker: Gnome
lines.text: Well if you don't need anything else I'd like to be left in peace.

sections.title: Speaking to Elena with full mourner's gear

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: How dare you enter my house, Mourner! Get out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Elena, it's me... [player name].

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: [player name]? I didn't recogonise you in all that Mourner gear!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, didn't mean to scare you.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I worry about what you've got yourself involved in. You be careful out there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll look after myself Elena. I promise.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You'd better. Let me know as soon as you work out what's going on.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey, how are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good thanks, yourself?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Not bad, did you speak to the king?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did, he admitted the plague is a hoax.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Did he say why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He did. Turns out he was lying to me though.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Lying? About what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About his loyalty. It seems the plague is part of some evil plan of his. I still don't know what that is though.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: This doesn't sound good at all. Should we tell someone?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I still need to work out exactly what's going on. I have some new allies who might help me with that though.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Well let me know as soon as you have something. And look after yourself out there, I worry about what you've got yourself involved in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be careful Elena. I promise.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: How goes it [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I've managed to infiltrate the mourners. I just have to find out what they are up to now.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Great work! Stick with it [player name].

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Have you finished with those sheep yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, it is done. What next?

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: It is good to see your enthusiasm. I was going to get one of the others to do this job, but as you are here... It has been quite some time since anyone got ill form the plague, so I would like you to see to it that people do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err... But the plague doesn't exist, how am I meant to do that?

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: We have never tried this before, but some joker put something in our food not too long ago that gave us all symptoms akin to those of the plague. If you can find out what it was that we were poisoned with, you could reproduce the effects. If done right the poison should not be fatal and could help restock our dwindling supply of cheap labour. We can have our men remove the 'infected' citizens and sent into the mines. Distribution should be easy enough. Because of the city walls no one can grow their own food. Instead, all the food here comes from one of three supply points.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let me get this clear, you want me to find out who poisoned you, find out what they poisoned you with, find out how to make the poison and produce enough poison to affect a lot of people?

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Don't forget the part where you use the poison to contaminate the food supply. Two of the three supply points should be enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How am I meant to do all of that?

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: You seem resourceful so I'm sure you will come up with something. I would help you but I am not a biologist.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Luckily for me I know a biologist nearby.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Someone trustworthy I hope?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, definitely.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Very well. Perform this task and then return to me.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: How goes it [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I've managed to infiltrate the mourners. I just have to find out what they are up to now.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Great work! Stick with it [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again Elena.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: How dare you enter my house, Mourner! Get out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Elena, it's me... [player name].

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: [player name]? I didn't recognise you in all that Mourner gear!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a long story.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Well I'm not going anywhere. What's going on?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: [player name]! How's it going? Have you found out any more about the plague?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As it goes, yes I have. It's a long story.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Well I'm not going anywhere. What's going on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'll start from when we found out that the plague was a hoax. When I confronted the king, he told me that he faked the plague to keep people safe from his brother, King Tyras. He said that Tyras was taken by the Dark Lord while exploring the lands to the west. He claimed that the Dark Lord corrupted him by forcing him to drink from the Chalice of Eternity.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: But that was a lie?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed, but I didn't find out until much later. On the kings orders, I travelled through the Underground Pass and into the western lands of Tirannwn. Once there, I met up with Lord Iorwerth, the leader of some elves that had allied with the king. He helped me kill King Tyras. That was when I discovered the truth. While returning to King Lathas, I was confronted by another elf named Arianwyn, the leader of a group of rebel elves opposing Lord Iorwerth. At the time, I was carrying a letter from Lord Iorwerth to King Lathas. Arianwyn magically broke the seal on the letter so I could read it.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: What did it say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It revealed that it's actually King Lathas and Lord Iorwerth who serve the Dark Lord. According to the letter, King Lathas wants to reclaim Camelot from King Arthur and believes the Dark Lord can help him.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: And Lord Iorwerth?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not exactly sure yet. West Ardougne seems to be key to his plans though. I met with Arianwyn who revealed to me that the mourners are actually elves in service to Lord Iorwerth. I've infiltrated the mourners and I'm trying to work out what they are doing. From what I've learnt so far, there's something important to them in the caves below West Ardougne. I'm currently working with them to earn their trust. Hopefully they'll reveal their plans to me soon.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I see, that's quite the reveal. If you need anything from me just ask.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I was hoping you could help me with something.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: What's the problem?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been asked to produce a poison based on rotten apples.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I doubt any poison based solely on apples, rotten or not, would be very effective.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I put a rotten apple in the Mourners' stew, I'm told that the effect was much like the symptoms of the plague.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hmm... That sounds like they were ill from some sort of toxin, I should think it was a mould of some sort that had the effect, not the rotten apple itself. What do you need it for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to poison a large food supply in order to get the Mourners' trust.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: That's awful, I can't help you with that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I don't gain the trust of the Mourners, then the people of West Ardougne will have a much worse time than the effects of that toxin. Elena trust me.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You'd better be right about this, and I'd better make sure you get this toxin right so no one dies! Bring me a sample of rotten apple to examine. I'll also see if I can make a counteractant for the toxin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright I'll be back shortly with a rotten apple for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have one right here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm back.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You have a sample of rotten apple for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I forgot to get one.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Well if you bring me one we can get started on this toxin.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Is that you [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes it's me.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You have a sample of rotten apple for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I have it right here.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Ick... Alright then let's get started.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Right.. lets see...

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Okay, I've managed to isolate a small sample of the toxin. It's a byproduct of the mould that grows on these apples, it's not fatal so a counteractant shouldn't be necessary. How big is the store that you're going to affect?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I was instructed to contaminate two of the three supply points in West Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: That's over half of the food in the city! You're going to need a huge amount of the toxin to do that. You're also going to need to refine it too, or people will notice rotten apples in amongst the food.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is starting to sound a little tricky, can't you make it for me?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I would, but I don't have the right equipment here to do anything in bulk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright then, tell me the process and I'll get started.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Right then, the first thing to do is mash up a lot of rotten apples, then you will need to dissolve the toxin into a liquid that has a very low evaporation point, some form of solvent. I don't know as much about those kinds of chemicals but I believe naphtha will be perfect for this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes, I've worked with naphtha before. The chemist in Rimmington helped me make it from coal tar.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Perfect. Well get yourself some naphtha and some mashed rotten apples and mix them together. Once you've done that, strain out any solids. Finally, heat the mixture to evaporate off the solvent. Be careful of naked flames as the solvent will be highly flammable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And that's all is it? Why isn't anything ever easy?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Sorry, I never said it would be simple. Here, you'll need this to strain out the solids.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You may want to check out the orchard just north of the city. I hear no one has tended it since the blight infected the trees there. I imagine it will have plenty of rotten apples.

sections.title: Talking to Elena with full mourner's gear again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Elena.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Huh? Oh hello. I just can't get used to a friend being dressed as mourner.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Elena.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: So how's the poisoning going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not too well, can you remind me how I should make it again?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Right, the first thing to do is mash up a lot of rotten apples. You'll probably be able to do that at the orchard just north of the city. I hear no one has tended it since the blight infected the trees there. Next, you'll want to get yourself some naphtha and mix it with the rotten apples. You mentioned the chemist in Rimmingon helped you with naphtha before. You might want to speak to him again. Once that's done, use the sieve I gave you to strain out any solids. Finally, heat the mixture to evaporate off the solvent. Be careful of naked flames as the solvent will be highly flammable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks Elena.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: No problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Elena.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: So how's the poisoning going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's all done.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: So have you found out what those mourner are up to yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was just on my way back there.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Well let me know when you find out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will.

lines.speaker: Gnome
lines.text: What are you after now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing I'm just talking to people randomly in case it helps.

lines.speaker: Gnome
lines.text: Does it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really, no.

lines.speaker: Gnome
lines.text: Maybe you should give me more toad crunchies. Ask any gnome, 'toad should be in everything' they'll tell you, mark my words.

sections.title: Use toad crunchies on the gnome

lines.speaker: Gnome
lines.text: Thanks... Toads always hit the spot!

sections.title: Use anything else on the gnome

lines.speaker: Gnome
lines.text: I got no need for that, keep it.

sections.title: Talking to the Head Mourner again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have the key to the mines yet?

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: No, the guard is being a bit slow, but we should have it soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll report in again later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Elena.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hello [player name]. Have you found out what those mourners are up to yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently they are trying to find an old temple deep under the city. That's what their mining operation is for. The temple seems to be the key to their plans. It apparently holds a great power. I still have no idea what that is yet though.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Well let me know when you find out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: How goes it [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I have the information you're after. It seems Iorwerth elves are searching for an ancient temple of some sort deep beneath West Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Temple? That must be the Temple of Light!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Temple of Light? What's the Temple of Light?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: It was built by Seren herself to guard a dark and ancient power. This is not good friend, I fear Lord Iorwerth intends to use that power to summon the Dark Lord!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think they've found this temple yet, but they know it's down there.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Well then we still have time. Thank you for your help so far, we would never have discovered this without you. Now we must prepare for what comes next.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what are we going to do about the Temple of Light?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: First of all, we need to know how close the Iorwerth elves are to finding it. Do you think you are ready for this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I am.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Well don't take too long. Every second we waste is a big risk.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: You are back already? How is the epidemic going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The epidemic? Oh, you mean the poisoning?

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Subtle as a brick... Yes that, how is it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's all done.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: This is good news, give it a few days and the slave pens will be full again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So have I proved my commitment now?

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Yes I think you have. I guess you want to know what we're doing in the mines?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: A long time ago, Seren herself ordered the construction of a great temple deep beneath the earth. This temple guards a great power, one that is key to our plans. We believe the temple lies beneath this city. That is why we are here. Unfortunately, progress has been slower than we'd have liked. The slaves accidently mined into some old caverns infested with beasts. They have caused us significant issues.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. But how close are we to finding it ?

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: It shouldn't be long now. We have started to see signs that we are near. Anyway, now that you know about the temple, I have a new task for you to perform, deep within the mines. Alas, one of the guards has taken the key to the mines to be copied. Report in regularly and I will see that you get a copy as soon as he gets back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Now be on your way, I have other things to attend to.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: What was that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh nothing.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Hmm, fair enough.
